name: repositories
author: dojo-community
context: Add, inspect, or remove APT repositories safely with deb822 source files, Signed-By keyrings, and PPA support
info: This skill manages repository configuration on Debian-family hosts. It covers deb822 `.sources` files, legacy `.list` lines, keyring placement under `/etc/apt/keyrings` or `/usr/share/keyrings`, explicit `Signed-By` wiring, and repository removal. It also routes Ubuntu Launchpad PPAs to the dedicated [[apt/repositories/ppa]] sub-skill. Agents use this node when a package is missing from the default repositories, when vendor documentation still suggests deprecated `apt-key` patterns, or when a host needs controlled third-party repository lifecycle management.

sections.title: Deb822 source files
sections.body: Deb822 `.sources` files are the preferred modern format. They support multiline stanzas, explicit `Signed-By`, and clearer structure than one-line entries. Ubuntu's own `ubuntu.sources` file is the reference example on current releases.

sections.title: Keyrings and Signed-By
sections.body: APT archive authentication should be bound explicitly with `Signed-By`. Debian documents `/etc/apt/keyrings` for locally managed keys and `/usr/share/keyrings` for package-managed keyrings. Avoid `apt-key`; Ubuntu and Debian both now route best practice through per-repository keyrings.

sections.title: Removing repositories cleanly
sections.body: Deleting the source file is only part of removal. Also remove repository-specific pins, optionally delete a manually installed keyring, and then run `apt-get update`. If third-party packages remain installed, expect candidate selection or upgrade behavior to stay influenced by that repository until those packages are downgraded, removed, or repinned.

sections.title: Ubuntu PPAs
sections.body: Use [[apt/repositories/ppa]] for Launchpad PPAs. `add-apt-repository` handles the shortcut syntax and key acquisition, but Ubuntu notes that it does not automatically configure repository pinning. Add pins manually when a PPA should not outrank official packages.

links.context: Reduce the priority of third-party repositories after adding them
Verify candidate selection after adding a new source
Install packages once the new repository metadata is available
Debug Signed-By and repository authentication failures

related.note: Pinning is the normal follow-up when third-party repositories should stay lower priority
The PPA sub-skill is the Ubuntu-specific repository shortcut workflow

scripts.name: Manage APT repository
scripts.description: Create, inspect, or remove APT source files and associated keyrings

APT_REPO_ACTION.description: Repository action: add-deb822, add-line, remove, or list

APT_REPO_NAME.description: Short repository name used for source and keyring filenames

APT_REPO_SUITES.description: Suite or suites, space-separated, such as stable or noble-updates

APT_REPO_COMPONENTS.description: Space-separated components such as main, contrib, or non-free

APT_REPO_ARCH.description: Optional architectures value for the source definition

APT_REPO_KEY_URL.description: URL to download a repository keyring or armored key

APT_REPO_SIGNED_BY.description: Explicit Signed-By path; defaults to /etc/apt/keyrings/<name>.asc or .gpg if a key URL is provided

APT_ENABLE_SOURCE.description: Set to true to add deb-src alongside deb

APT_NO_UPDATE.description: Set to true to skip apt-get update after changes

APT_REMOVE_KEY.description: Set to true to delete a matching keyring on repository removal

APT_REMOVE_PIN.description: Set to true to delete matching preference files on repository removal

name.description: Short name used for source and keyring filenames

keyringPath.description: Keyring path associated with the repository

depends.reason: Third-party repositories are often paired with explicit pinning to avoid overtaking official packages

body:
# Managing APT Repositories

Use this node when packages are not available from the current sources or when a host needs to add, inspect, or remove an external repository. Modern APT repository management centers on explicit keyrings and deb822 source files.

## Preferred deb822 format

```bash
curl -fsSL https://packages.vendor.example/key.asc -o /tmp/vendor.asc
sudo install -d -m 0755 /etc/apt/keyrings
sudo install -m 0644 /tmp/vendor.asc /etc/apt/keyrings/vendor.asc

cat <<'EON' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/vendor.sources >/dev/null
Types: deb
URIs: https://packages.vendor.example/apt
Suites: stable
Components: main
Signed-By: /etc/apt/keyrings/vendor.asc
EON

sudo apt-get update
```

Debian documents `.sources` as the modern format. Ubuntu 24.04 and newer also default to deb822 source files for their official repositories.

## Legacy one-line format

```bash
echo "deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/vendor.asc] https://packages.vendor.example/apt stable main" | \
  sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/vendor.list >/dev/null
sudo apt-get update
```

Legacy `.list` files are still supported, but Debian documents them as deprecated in favor of deb822.

## Keyrings and Signed-By

APT archive authentication should use explicit keyrings rather than `apt-key`. The current best practice is:

- local administrator-managed keys in `/etc/apt/keyrings`
- package-managed archive keyrings in `/usr/share/keyrings`
- `Signed-By` in the source definition to bind the repository to that keyring

See [[apt/pinning]] if you need to reduce precedence for a third-party repository after adding it.

## Removing a repository

```bash
sudo rm -f /etc/apt/sources.list.d/vendor.sources
sudo rm -f /etc/apt/preferences.d/vendor
sudo rm -f /etc/apt/keyrings/vendor.asc
sudo apt-get update
```

If packages from the repository are already installed, remove or repin them intentionally before deleting the source. Ubuntu explicitly warns that third-party repositories can interfere with release upgrades if they are left unmanaged.

## PPAs

Launchpad PPAs are a special Ubuntu-only shortcut. Use [[apt/repositories/ppa]] when a task explicitly references `ppa:user/name` or `add-apt-repository`.
